Notes on paper complexity (if applicable: Paper is relatively simple and brief, as it only reports a single proband.
Clinical presentation (HPO): Spasticity (HP:0001257), Global Developmental Delay (HP:0001263), Facial Dysmorphism (HP:0001999), Epileptic Spasm (HP:0011097), Central Hypotonia (HP:0001252), Hypsarrhythmia (HP:0002521), Molar Tooth Sign of Midbrain (HP:0002419), Cerebellar Vermis Hypoplasia (HP:0001320),
Clinical presentation (free text): The proband had episodic spasms of the trunk along with stiffness of the limbs lasting for 1-2 and accompanied by arching of the back. These episodes occurred more frequently when the individual was awake, and often happened in clusters with greater frequency in the past month. The developmental age was 2-3 months. Facial dysmorphism was subtle, and central hypotonia was mild.. Prominent cerebellar folia and a prominent fourth ventricle on brain imaging were present. The hypsarrhytmia showed full remission. The proband was diagnosed with West Syndrome.

Notes on paper complexity (if applicable: This article reports a homozygous knockout mouse model, not any human cases.
Additional methods (including previous t: The homozygous knockout mice exhibit decreased cardiac function before birth that is progressive to eccentric cardiac growth, ventricular dilation, and worsening cardiac function in adulthood. Also, the mice have significantly decreased cardiac contratility and increased remodeling due to pressure overload as a result of transverse aortic constriction.

Notes on paper complexity (if applicable: This article reports a mouse model of the NGLN3 variant p.Arg451Cys, which has been identified in patients with autism spectrum disorder.
Additional methods (including previous t: Mice with the p.Arg451Cys monoallelic variant have an increased inhibition to excitation (I/E) balance of synaptic inputs to Purkinje cells. Calcium transients in the soma caused by high strength and low strength climbing fiber inputs were decreased in the mutants. Thus, the findings indicate that an individual missense variant substantially impacts the synapse development in regulation of cerebelllar circuitry. This could be part of the mechanism behind autism spectrum disorder.
